Advisory/Declarations/MissingOverrideAnn.checker_desc: Detects Java methods that override a superclass method but are missing the @Override annotation.

Advisory/Deprecated Code/AvoidDeprecated.checker_desc: Detects invocations of deprecated methods or constructors from non-deprecated code.

Advisory/Documentation/SpuriousJavadocPa.checker_desc: Detects Javadoc @param tags that do not correspond to any actual parameter or type parameter in the associated method, constructor, class, interface, or record.

Advisory/Documentation/ImpossibleJavadoc.checker_desc: Detects Javadoc @throws tags that reference a checked exception type not declared in the method's throws clause.

DeadCode/UselessParameter.ql.checker_desc: This checker detects parameters in Java methods or constructors that are never accessed within the method body, indicating dead code.

Language Abuse/UselessNullCheck.ql.checker_desc: This checker detects null-check expressions that are useless because the checked expression is guaranteed to be non-null at that point.

Language Abuse/IterableIterator.ql.checker_desc: This checker detects classes that implement Iterable by returning themselves as the Iterator but lack a guard to prevent multiple concurrent iterations.

Language Abuse/UselessTypeTest.ql.checker_desc: Detects unnecessary instanceof checks where the tested expression's type is guaranteed to be a subtype of the checked type.

Language Abuse/LabelInSwitch.ql.checker_desc: Detects a labeled statement (non-case label) appearing inside a switch statement, which is confusing or potentially erroneous.

Language Abuse/WrappedIterator.ql.checker_desc: This checker detects classes that implement Iterable but do not support multiple iterations because their iterator method always returns the same iterator instance, either directly or wrapped.

Language Abuse/ChainedInstanceof.ql.checker_desc: Detects if statements that form a chain of more than 5 consecutive instanceof type tests on a variable.

Likely Bugs/Nullness/NullExprDeref.ql.checker_desc: This checker detects expressions that are dereferenced and may evaluate to null at runtime.

Likely Bugs/Nullness/NullAlways.ql.checker_desc: This checker detects variable dereferences where the variable is always null at the point of dereference.

Likely Bugs/Nullness/NullMaybe.ql.checker_desc: This checker detects potential null pointer dereferences where a variable may be null at a dereference point, but is not guaranteed to always be null.

Likely Bugs/Concurrency/NonSynchronizedO.checker_desc: This checker detects non-synchronized Java methods that override a synchronized method without being synchronized themselves, unless they only delegate to the super method via a simple super call.

Likely Bugs/Concurrency/CallsToRunnableR.checker_desc: This checker detects direct calls to the `run()` method of a Thread subclass instead of calling `start()` to spawn a new thread.

Likely Bugs/Concurrency/SynchOnBoxedType.checker_desc: This checker detects synchronization statements where the synchronized expression's type is a boxed primitive type or the String type.

Likely Bugs/Concurrency/DoubleCheckedLoc.checker_desc: This checker detects a potential race condition in double-checked locking patterns where a field assignment inside a synchronized block may be visible to other threads before subsequent side-effect statements are executed.
Detects double-checked locking patterns on non-volatile fields that are not thread-safe due to improper synchronization and field visibility issues.

Likely Bugs/Concurrency/Escaping.ql.checker_desc: This checker detects non-final, non-private, non-volatile fields in classes annotated as thread-safe that may escape proper synchronization, potentially causing concurrency issues.

Likely Bugs/Concurrency/SynchSetUnsynchG.checker_desc: This checker detects unsynchronized getter methods that correspond to synchronized setter methods in the same class, where both methods do not access a volatile field.

Likely Bugs/Concurrency/ScheduledThreadP.checker_desc: Detects when `ScheduledThreadPoolExecutor` is configured with zero threads via constructor or `setCorePoolSize` method.

Likely Bugs/Concurrency/ThreadSafe.ql.checker_desc: This checker detects fields in classes annotated as @ThreadSafe that are not properly synchronized, either because they have publicly accessible accesses not protected by any monitor, or because no single monitor covers all accesses to the field.

Likely Bugs/Statements/PartiallyMaskedCa.checker_desc: This checker detects unreachable catch clauses in a try statement where a more specific exception type is caught after a more general one, making the specific catch clause unreachable.

Likely Bugs/Statements/ContinueInFalseLo.checker_desc: This checker detects a 'continue' statement inside a 'do' loop whose condition is always false, meaning the continue will never actually re-run the loop body.

Likely Bugs/Inheritance/NoNonFinalInCons.checker_desc: This checker detects a call from a superclass constructor to a non-abstract, non-final method that may be overridden by a subclass, where the overriding method reads a non-final field that is only written in a subclass constructor, potentially causing the field to be read before it is initialized.

Likely Bugs/Likely Typos/MissingSpaceTyp.checker_desc: Detects string literals that are concatenated at compile-time and likely missing a space between words.

Likely Bugs/Likely Typos/ContainerSizeCm.checker_desc: This checker detects comparisons of container size (array length, string length, collection size, or map size) to zero that are always true or always false because container sizes cannot be negative.

Likely Bugs/Likely Typos/UnusedFormatArg.checker_desc: Detects format method calls where the format string references fewer arguments than supplied, leading to unused arguments.

Likely Bugs/Likely Typos/SelfAssignment..checker_desc: This checker detects assignment expressions where the destination variable and source variable refer to the same variable, indicating a likely self-assignment typo.

Likely Bugs/Likely Typos/ContradictoryTy.checker_desc: This checker detects contradictory type checks where a variable is first checked to be of a supertype via an instanceof guard, and later accessed with a cast or instanceof check for a subtype, which is impossible.

Likely Bugs/Likely Typos/MissingFormatAr.checker_desc: This checker detects format method calls where the number of format specifiers in the format string exceeds the number of supplied arguments.

Likely Bugs/Likely Typos/StringBufferCha.checker_desc: This checker detects when a character value is passed as the first argument to a new StringBuffer or StringBuilder constructor, which is interpreted as the initial capacity rather than a character to append.

Likely Bugs/Likely Typos/SuspiciousDateF.checker_desc: This checker detects suspicious date format patterns passed to SimpleDateFormat constructors that likely contain typos, specifically patterns containing both "%Y" and "%M".

Likely Bugs/Arithmetic/ConstantExpAppear.checker_desc: This checker detects expressions that always evaluate to the same constant value but are not recognized as compile-time constants by the compiler, often due to containing non-constant sub-expressions that don't affect the final result.

Likely Bugs/Arithmetic/WhitespaceContrad.checker_desc: This checker detects nested binary operators where the whitespace around the inner operator suggests a higher precedence than the outer operator, contradicting the actual precedence rules.

Likely Bugs/Arithmetic/LShiftLargerThanT.checker_desc: This checker detects left-shift expressions where the shift amount (right operand) is a compile-time constant integer that is greater than or equal to the bit width of the left operand's integral type, which causes the shift amount to be truncated modulo the type width.

Likely Bugs/Arithmetic/IntMultToLong.ql.checker_desc: This checker detects integer multiplication expressions that may overflow before being implicitly widened to a larger integral type in a conversion context.

Likely Bugs/Arithmetic/InformationLoss.q.checker_desc: This checker detects implicit narrowing casts in compound assignment operators (like += or *=) where the source expression's numeric type is wider than the destination variable's type, potentially causing information loss.

Likely Bugs/Resource Leaks/CloseWriter.q.checker_desc: This checker detects instances where a Writer or OutputStream is created but not guaranteed to be closed on method exit, potentially causing resource leaks.

Likely Bugs/Resource Leaks/CloseSql.ql.checker_desc: This checker detects SQL resource objects (Connection, Statement, ResultSet) that are initialized locally and not guaranteed to be closed on method exit.

Likely Bugs/Resource Leaks/CloseReader.q.checker_desc: This checker detects instances of Reader, InputStream, or ZipFile objects that are created but not guaranteed to be closed on method exit, potentially causing resource leaks.

Likely Bugs/Comparison/CompareIdenticalV.checker_desc: Detects comparisons where the left and right operands are identical in value, such as comparing the same variable, literal, or structurally equal expressions.

Likely Bugs/Comparison/MissingInstanceof.checker_desc: This checker detects equals() methods that likely have a bug because they do not perform a type test (e.g., instanceof) on the method parameter before performing operations that assume it is of the correct type.

Likely Bugs/Comparison/EqualsArray.ql.checker_desc: Detects calls to equals or hashCode methods on array objects, which compare object identity rather than array contents.

Likely Bugs/Comparison/InconsistentEqual.checker_desc: This checker detects classes that override either equals or hashCode but not the other, violating the contract that these two methods should be consistently overridden together.

Likely Bugs/Comparison/UselessComparison.checker_desc: This checker detects binary comparison expressions (like <, >, ==, !=) that are always true or always false based on constant value analysis, overflow detection, or guarded equality tests, excluding those used in assertions, overflow tests, or concurrent modification checks.

Likely Bugs/Comparison/IncomparableEqual.checker_desc: Detects calls to equals() where the receiver type and argument type are provably incomparable (no common subtype).

Likely Bugs/Comparison/WrongNanCompariso.checker_desc: This checker detects equality comparisons involving a direct access to a static NaN field of a floating-point type, which will always yield the same result (false for ==, true for !=) because NaN is not equal to itself.

Likely Bugs/Comparison/HashedButNoHash.q.checker_desc: This checker detects when a type that defines an equals method but no hashCode method is used in a hashing data structure (like HashSet or HashMap).

Likely Bugs/Comparison/RefEqBoxed.ql.checker_desc: This checker detects suspicious reference equality comparisons (== or !=) between two operands that are both boxed numeric types, excluding comparisons involving java.lang.Boolean.

Likely Bugs/Collections/ArrayIndexOutOfB.checker_desc: This checker detects array accesses where the index might be equal to or exceed the array length, indicating a potential ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException.

Likely Bugs/Collections/WriteOnlyContain.checker_desc: Detects container variables (collections/maps) whose contents are never queried or accessed after initialization.

Likely Bugs/Collections/ReadOnlyContaine.checker_desc: This checker detects container variables (collections or maps) that are declared and possibly assigned fresh instances but are only ever used for read-only query operations, suggesting their contents are never initialized.

Likely Bugs/Collections/ContainsTypeMism.checker_desc: This checker detects calls to Java collection methods where the argument type is incompatible with the collection's element type, such as calling `contains` with an argument that can never match any element in the collection.

Likely Bugs/Collections/RemoveTypeMismat.checker_desc: This checker detects calls to collection/container modification methods where the actual argument type is incompatible with the expected element type of the container, likely causing the removal operation to fail silently.

Likely Bugs/Termination/ConstantLoopCond.checker_desc: This checker detects loop conditions that are constant within the loop body, potentially leading to non-terminating loops.

Likely Bugs/Frameworks/JUnit/JUnit5Missi.checker_desc: This checker detects JUnit 5 inner test classes that are missing the required '@Nested' annotation.

Performance/NewStringString.ql.checker_desc: Detects inefficient instantiation of a String object using another String as the constructor argument.

Performance/InefficientKeySetIterator.ql.checker_desc: Detects inefficient map iteration using keySet iterator with subsequent get calls instead of using entrySet iterator.

Performance/InefficientPrimConstructor.q.checker_desc: Detects inefficient constructor calls for boxed primitive types where valueOf should be used instead.

Performance/InnerClassCouldBeStatic.ql.checker_desc: Detects non-static nested classes that could be declared static because they don't use their enclosing instance outside constructors.

Likely Bugs/Concurrency/SafePublication..checker_desc: This checker detects fields in classes annotated as thread-safe that are not safely published, meaning they lack proper synchronization mechanisms (final, static, volatile, thread-safe type, or default value assignment) to prevent visibility issues in concurrent access.

Likely Bugs/Statements/UseBraces.ql.checker_desc: This checker detects unbraced control flow bodies where the indentation of the next statement suggests it belongs to the control structure, but it does not, risking misinterpretation.

Performance/InefficientOutputStream.ql.checker_desc: This checker detects concrete classes that extend OutputStream or FilterOutputStream but only override the single-byte write(int) method while inheriting the inefficient three-parameter write(byte[],int,int) method from the superclass.

Performance/StringReplaceAllWithNonRegex.checker_desc: Detects calls to `String.replaceAll` where the first argument is a string literal containing only alphanumeric characters, indicating it's not a regular expression and should use `String.replace` instead.

Performance/InefficientEmptyStringTest.q.checker_desc: Detects inefficient empty string comparisons using `String.equals("")` or `"".equals(someString)` in Java code.

Security/CWE/CWE-200/SpringBootActuators.checker_desc: This checker detects when Spring Security configuration allows unauthenticated access to Spring Boot actuator endpoints via permitAll() calls.

Security/CWE/CWE-730/ReDoS.ql.checker_desc: This checker detects regular expression patterns that may cause exponential backtracking (ReDoS) due to ambiguous nested quantifiers or alternations inside infinite repetitions.

Security/CWE/CWE-295/ImproperWebViewCert.checker_desc: This checker detects Android WebViewClient.onReceivedSslError method implementations that unconditionally accept all SSL certificates by calling SslErrorHandler.proceed without proper validation.

Security/CWE/CWE-614/InsecureCookie.ql.checker_desc: This checker detects when a cookie is added to an HTTP response without the 'secure' flag being set, which could allow the cookie to be transmitted over unencrypted connections.

Security/CWE/CWE-335/PredictableSeed.ql.checker_desc: This checker detects usage of a SecureRandom number generator that is seeded with a predictable value, which can lead to insecure randomness.

Security/CWE/CWE-312/CleartextStorageCoo.checker_desc: This checker detects storing sensitive data in a cleartext cookie by identifying when sensitive data flows into a Cookie constructor argument and that cookie is subsequently added to an HttpServletResponse.

Security/CWE/CWE-113/NettyResponseSplitt.checker_desc: This checker detects Netty HTTP request/response splitting vulnerabilities where header value verification is disabled during the instantiation of certain Netty HTTP classes.

Security/CWE/CWE-209/StackTraceExposure..checker_desc: This checker detects when stack trace information from an exception is exposed to external users, either directly via printStackTrace calls to servlet writers or via stringified stack traces flowing to HTTP responses.

Security/CWE/CWE-209/SensitiveDataExposu.checker_desc: This checker detects when sensitive data from a Throwable.getMessage() call flows into an HTTP response, potentially exposing error information to external users.

Security/CWE/CWE-020/OverlyLargeRange.ql.checker_desc: This checker detects suspicious character ranges in Java regular expressions that are overly large, overlapping, or empty.

Security/CWE/CWE-925/ImproperIntentVerif.checker_desc: Detects Android broadcast receivers registered for system actions that do not verify incoming intents, making them susceptible to misuse by third-party apps.

Security/CWE/CWE-352/SpringCSRFProtectio.checker_desc: This checker detects calls that disable Spring Security's CSRF protection in web applications.

Security/CWE/CWE-338/JHipsterGeneratedPR.checker_desc: This checker detects JHipster RandomUtil methods that use Apache Commons RandomStringUtils methods (excluding "random") for generating strings, indicating a weak random number generator vulnerability (CVE-2019-16303).

Security/CWE/CWE-287/AndroidInsecureLoca.checker_desc: This checker detects Android authentication callback methods that do not use the authentication result parameter for any cryptographic operation.

Violations of Best Practice/Implementati.checker_desc: Detects casts from abstract collection interfaces to concrete implementation classes without proper type guarding.
Detects methods that accidentally expose internal representation by returning or storing into fields of array, Map, or Collection types, allowing external modification.

Violations of Best Practice/Records/Igno.checker_desc: This checker detects any member of a record class that has a name matching one of the special serialization-related method or field names, which are ignored by the Java serialization mechanism for records.

Violations of Best Practice/SpecialChara.checker_desc: This checker detects string or numeric literals containing non-explicit control characters, non-printable whitespace characters, or the zero-width space character (U+200B) in Java source files.

Violations of Best Practice/Declarations.checker_desc: Detects types that implement or extend an interface or abstract class solely for accessing its constant fields.

Violations of Best Practice/Boxed Types/.checker_desc: This checker detects local variables declared with a boxed type (like `Integer`) that are never assigned null and only hold primitive values, making the boxed type unnecessary.

Violations of Best Practice/Naming Conve.checker_desc: Detects overloaded methods with the same parameter count where each corresponding parameter type pair is convertible via casting, autoboxing, or type intersection, which may cause confusion.
Detects non-deprecated methods in the same class with names that differ only in letter capitalization.
Detects local variable declarations that shadow non-static fields in non-static methods/constructors, potentially causing confusion in code readability.
A class defined in source code has the same simple name as one of its supertypes.
Detects ambiguous unqualified method calls in nested types where the same method signature exists in both a superclass and an enclosing class.

Violations of Best Practice/Undesirable.checker_desc: Detects calls to `toString()` where the type inherits the default `Object.toString()` implementation.
Detects implicit array-to-string conversions that produce unreadable output in printing/formatting contexts.
Detects redundant `toString()` method calls on objects of type `String`.
This checker detects calls to the finalize() method that are not made via super.finalize() within an overriding finalize() method.

Violations of Best Practice/Testing/Exce.checker_desc: Detects JUnit 4 test methods that mock every public method of a class or interface with two or more public methods.

Violations of Best Practice/Dead Code/De.checker_desc: Detects non-public classes or interfaces that are never referenced in the codebase and can be removed.

Violations of Best Practice/Dead Code/Un.checker_desc: Detects local variable declarations that are never read after being written.
This checker detects labeled statements in Java code whose label is never referenced by any jump statement (break or continue).

Violations of Best Practice/Dead Code/In.checker_desc: Detects interface methods that conflict with protected Object methods due to incompatible return types, making the interface unimplementable.

Violations of Best Practice/Exception Ha.checker_desc: Detects string-to-number conversion expressions that may throw NumberFormatException without being caught or declared.
This checker detects method calls that discard the return value of specific methods known to return exceptional status codes, where ignoring such values could lead to undetected failures.

Security/CWE/CWE-732/ReadingFromWorldWri.checker_desc: This checker detects when a file variable is set as world-writable and later read from, creating a security vulnerability where sensitive data could be modified by unauthorized users.

Advisory/Statements/MissingDefaultInSwit.checker_desc: This checker detects switch statements that lack a default case and whose expression type is not an enum type.

DeadCode/DeadField.ql.checker_desc: This checker detects fields that are dead code, meaning they are either entirely unread or only read from dead code (including dead-code cycles).

Frameworks/JavaEE/EJB/EjbStaticFieldNonF.checker_desc: This checker detects when an EJB component accesses a non-final static field.

Language Abuse/EmptyMethod.ql.checker_desc: This checker detects methods that are completely empty, containing no executable statements, comments, annotations, or documentation.

Likely Bugs/Cloning/MissingCallToSuperCl.checker_desc: This checker detects clone methods that do not call super.clone() themselves but are overridden and called by a subclass's clone method.

Likely Bugs/Comparison/NoAssignInBoolean.checker_desc: This checker detects assignments that appear within boolean expressions but are not part of a comparison or equality test.

Likely Bugs/Comparison/StringComparison..checker_desc: This checker detects string comparison operations using reference equality (== or !=) where at least one of the compared string values is not guaranteed to be interned.

Likely Bugs/Concurrency/CallsToCondition.checker_desc: This checker detects calls to Object.wait() on objects that implement java.util.concurrent.locks.Condition, where Condition.await() should be used instead.

Likely Bugs/Concurrency/LazyInitStaticFi.checker_desc: This checker detects incorrect lazy initialization of static fields where the null-check and assignment are not properly synchronized, potentially causing race conditions in multi-threaded environments.

Likely Bugs/Concurrency/NotifyNotNotifyA.checker_desc: This checker detects calls to the `notify` method (without arguments) that are not qualified by `super` and are invoked on an object (not an interface).

Likely Bugs/Concurrency/SleepWithLock.ql.checker_desc: This checker detects calls to `Thread.sleep()` that occur within a synchronized method or a synchronized statement block.

Likely Bugs/Concurrency/SynchWriteObject.checker_desc: This checker detects a synchronized writeObject method in a serializable class where no other synchronized method is inherited by the class.

Likely Bugs/Concurrency/WaitWithTwoLocks.checker_desc: This checker detects calls to `Object.wait()` that occur while the current thread holds two locks due to nested synchronization.

Likely Bugs/Likely Typos/DangerousNonCir.checker_desc: This checker detects bitwise AND/OR operators used with boolean operands where the right operand is a dangerous expression that could have side effects, potentially causing unintended behavior due to non-short-circuit evaluation.

Likely Bugs/Serialization/NonSerializabl.checker_desc: This checker detects non-transient, non-static fields in serializable classes whose types are not serializable, excluding certain exceptions like compiler-generated classes or those with custom serialization methods.

Likely Bugs/Statements/MissingEnumInSwit.checker_desc: This checker detects switch statements over an enum type that are missing cases for some enum constants and also lack a default case.

Likely Bugs/Statements/ReturnValueIgnore.checker_desc: This checker detects method calls where the return value is ignored, but the method is typically used for its return value in most other calls.

Security/CWE/CWE-079/AndroidWebViewAddJa.checker_desc: This checker detects calls to the Android WebView's addJavascriptInterface method, which can expose Java objects to JavaScript and potentially lead to security vulnerabilities.

Security/CWE/CWE-079/AndroidWebViewSetti.checker_desc: This checker detects calls to the method `setJavaScriptEnabled` with a `true` argument on instances of `android.webkit.WebSettings` in Android WebView configurations.

Security/CWE/CWE-1004/SensitiveCookieNot.checker_desc: This checker detects sensitive cookies (with names matching certain patterns) that are set in HTTP responses without the HttpOnly flag, which could expose them to client-side script access.

Security/CWE/CWE-200/AndroidWebViewSetti.checker_desc: This checker detects calls to specific Android WebSettings methods that enable cross-origin file access with a true argument, potentially allowing unauthorized access to sensitive information.

Security/CWE/CWE-312/CleartextStorageAnd.checker_desc: This checker detects storing sensitive data in a SQLite database without encryption, where the data originates from a sensitive source and is stored via specific database operations.
This checker detects when sensitive data is written to a local file on the Android filesystem without proper encryption.

Security/CWE/CWE-330/InsecureRandomness..checker_desc: This checker detects insecure randomness usage where data from an insecure random source flows to a sensitive sink without proper sanitization.

Security/CWE/CWE-470/FragmentInjectionIn.checker_desc: This checker detects exported Android PreferenceActivity subclasses where the isValidFragment method always returns true, making the activity vulnerable to Fragment Injection.

Violations of Best Practice/Comments/Tod.checker_desc: This checker detects TODO or FIXME comments in Java source code.

Violations of Best Practice/Dead Code/De.checker_desc: This checker detects assignments to local variables that are never read before being overwritten, indicating dead stores.

Violations of Best Practice/Dead Code/Em.checker_desc: This checker detects finalize methods in Java source code that are empty (contain no statements).

Violations of Best Practice/Declarations.checker_desc: Detects switch cases that may unintentionally fall through to the next case without a break, return, or a comment indicating intentional fallthrough.

Violations of Best Practice/Exception Ha.checker_desc: This checker detects catch clauses that catch overly broad exception types (Throwable or Exception) without using the caught variable and where the try block does not throw any exception of that specific type or its subtypes.

Violations of Best Practice/Undesirable.checker_desc: This checker detects calls to System.exit() or Runtime.halt() in Java source methods that are not main methods, test methods, or intentional exits via parameter passing.

Violations of Best Practice/legacy/Unnec.checker_desc: This checker detects casts where the expression being cast is already of the same type as the cast target, making the cast operation unnecessary.

Security/CWE/CWE-1204/StaticInitializati.checker_desc: This checker detects the use of a statically initialized byte array as an initialization vector (IV) for encryption, which can lead to predictable encryption and security vulnerabilities.

Security/CWE/CWE-297/UnsafeHostnameVerif.checker_desc: This checker detects hostname verifier implementations that always accept any certificate regardless of hostname mismatch, potentially enabling man-in-the-middle attacks.

Security/CWE/CWE-326/InsufficientKeySize.checker_desc: Detects cryptographic keys with insufficient bit sizes that flow from creation to usage without being adequately increased.

Security/CWE/CWE-522/InsecureBasicAuth.q.checker_desc: Detects data flow from insecure basic authentication sources (like hardcoded credentials) to authentication sinks (like HTTP requests).

Violations of Best Practice/Implementati.checker_desc: It detects illegal access from production code to a VisibleForTesting‑annotated element outside its allowed visibility scope.

Security/CWE/CWE-022/TaintedPath.ql.checker_desc: This checker detects path injection vulnerabilities where user-provided (tainted) data flows into file or path operations without proper sanitization.

Security/CWE/CWE-601/UrlRedirect.ql.checker_desc: This checker detects untrusted URL redirection vulnerabilities where user-provided input can flow to a URL redirection sink without proper sanitization.

Security/CWE/CWE-134/ExternallyControlle.checker_desc: This checker detects format string vulnerabilities where a format string argument in a formatting method call is externally controlled (tainted) from a user-provided source.

Security/CWE/CWE-502/UnsafeDeserializati.checker_desc: This checker detects unsafe deserialization where untrusted data flows to a deserialization sink without proper sanitization.

Security/CWE/CWE-807/TaintedPermissionsC.checker_desc: This checker detects when a permissions check (e.g., constructing a WildCardPermission or calling a specific method) uses a user-controlled value as input, creating a tainted permissions vulnerability.

Security/CWE/CWE-022/ZipSlip.ql.checker_desc: This checker detects unsanitized archive entry names (e.g., from ZipEntry.getName()) flowing into file system operations, potentially allowing Zip Slip attacks through directory traversal sequences like "..".

Security/CWE/CWE-094/SpelInjection.ql.checker_desc: This checker detects potential SpEL injection vulnerabilities where user-provided data flows into a Spring Expression Language (SpEL) evaluation method without proper sanitization.

Security/CWE/CWE-094/InsecureBeanValidat.checker_desc: This checker detects when a custom Bean Validation constraint error message contains unsanitized user input that could lead to Expression Language injection.

Security/CWE/CWE-730/RegexInjection.ql.checker_desc: This checker detects regular expression injection vulnerabilities where user-provided input flows unsanitized into a regular expression pattern compilation.

Security/CWE/CWE-094/JexlInjection.ql.checker_desc: This checker detects JEXL expression injection vulnerabilities where user-provided data flows unsafely into JEXL evaluation methods.

Security/CWE/CWE-730/PolynomialReDoS.ql.checker_desc: This checker detects regular expressions with polynomial backtracking complexity that are matched against user-provided input without proper length restrictions, potentially causing ReDoS vulnerabilities.

Security/CWE/CWE-681/NumericCastTainted..checker_desc: This checker detects numeric narrowing casts (e.g., int to byte) where the cast expression is a variable access that is tainted by a user-provided source, without passing through a sanitizing barrier.

Security/CWE/CWE-078/ExecTainted.ql.checker_desc: This checker detects command injection vulnerabilities where user-controlled input flows unsanitized into a command execution sink.

Security/CWE/CWE-780/RsaWithoutOaep.ql.checker_desc: This checker detects when an RSA cipher algorithm specification string that does not contain "OAEP" flows to a cryptographic algorithm specification parameter.

Security/CWE/CWE-552/UrlForward.ql.checker_desc: This checker detects untrusted URL forward vulnerabilities where user-provided input flows to a URL forward sink without proper sanitization against path injection.

Security/CWE/CWE-089/SqlTainted.ql.checker_desc: This checker detects SQL injection vulnerabilities where user-provided data flows unsanitized into a database query sink.

Security/CWE/CWE-489/WebviewDebuggingEna.checker_desc: This checker detects when a true boolean literal flows to the argument of `WebView.setWebContentsDebuggingEnabled` without being blocked by a debug-check guard or being inside a non-security test class.

Security/CWE/CWE-074/XsltInjection.ql.checker_desc: This checker detects XSLT injection vulnerabilities where user-controlled input flows into an XSLT stylesheet source parameter without proper sanitization.

Security/CWE/CWE-327/BrokenCryptoAlgorit.checker_desc: This checker detects insecure cryptographic algorithms (e.g., DES, RC4, ECB mode) being used as the algorithm specification in cryptographic operations.

Security/CWE/CWE-940/AndroidIntentRedire.checker_desc: This checker detects Android intent redirection vulnerabilities where user-provided data can flow to an intent that starts arbitrary activities or services.

Security/CWE/CWE-927/ImplicitPendingInte.checker_desc: This checker detects when an implicit Intent is created and then flows into a PendingIntent that is sent to an unspecified third party.

Security/CWE/CWE-113/ResponseSplitting.q.checker_desc: This checker detects potential HTTP response splitting vulnerabilities where user-provided data containing newline/carriage return characters flows into HTTP header values without proper sanitization.
